notes: price_now is the May 15 intraday price; May 14 confirmed close was $443.30. May 15 closes the week on a bullish note.

news.sentiment_summary: Tesla sentiment in mid-May 2026 is firmly bullish, powered by three converging catalysts: a decisive Q1 earnings beat in April, an accelerating robotaxi rollout now active in Dallas and Houston, and high-stakes diplomatic momentum around China FSD approval during Elon Musk's state visit to Beijing alongside President Trump. The stock has gained over 24% in 30 days. Near-term turbulence on May 12 — sparked by robotaxi operational glitches and Panasonic battery supply concerns — was absorbed within 48 hours, underlining strong dip-buying demand. The dominant market narrative has firmly shifted from EV-maker commodity concerns to AI, autonomy, and robotics optimism.

items.headline: Musk Joins Trump's Beijing State Visit — China FSD Approval Could Unlock Massive Revenue
items.summary: Elon Musk accompanied President Trump on an official state visit to Beijing, meeting with Xi Jinping and regulators. Tesla executives expect FSD regulatory approval in China sometime in Q3 2026 after an earlier window passed without clearance — a multi-billion dollar software subscription opportunity that could re-rate the stock significantly if greenlit.

items.headline: Tesla Q1 2026 Earnings Beat: EPS $0.41 vs $0.36 Estimate, Gross Margin Expands to 21.1%
items.summary: Tesla beat Q1 2026 consensus estimates with non-GAAP EPS of $0.41 versus the $0.36 expected, and revenue of $22.39B (+16% YoY). Automotive gross margin reached 21.1% — the highest in over a year — while FSD active subscriptions grew 51% year-over-year to 1.28 million. Higher capex guidance of $25B+ for the full year (up $5B from prior guidance) was the main drag on post-earnings sentiment.

items.headline: Piper Sandler: Tesla's Core Business Worth ~$400; Optimus Robot Is 'Free' at Current Prices
items.summary: Analyst Alexander Potter at Piper Sandler argued that Tesla's 17 core product lines — from vehicles and energy storage to FSD subscriptions and robotaxi — justify around $400 per share alone. At current prices near $445–449, investors are effectively getting the Optimus humanoid robot program at no additional cost. The firm reiterated a $500 price target.

items.headline: Tesla Sinks 5% Intraday on Robotaxi Glitches and Panasonic Battery Delay Reports
items.summary: TSLA fell as much as 5% intraday on May 12, closing down 2.6% at $433.45, after reports of long wait times and ride cancellations in Dallas and Houston robotaxi zones. Panasonic battery supply constraints are also throttling Cybercab and Tesla Semi production ramps, adding execution risk to the autonomous vehicle timeline. The stock fully recovered within two sessions.

items.headline: Tesla Robotaxi Fleet Grows to 39 Vehicles — Five More Cities Queued for H1 2026 Launch
items.summary: Tesla's unsupervised robotaxi fleet has grown from 9 vehicles in early April 2026 to 39 by mid-May, with Dallas and Houston zones live since April 18. Paid robotaxi miles nearly doubled quarter-over-quarter per Q1 disclosure. Phoenix, Miami, Orlando, Tampa, and Las Vegas are next in queue, with 12 US states targeted by year-end 2026.

technical_signals: All 12 moving averages (MA5 through MA200) signal Buy — zero bearish signals across all timeframes
RSI at ~70 — approaching overbought territory; momentum is strong but stretched
Price above 5-day MA ($445), 50-day MA ($416), and 200-day MA ($383) — bullish 'golden stack'
Key support zone: $428–$435; immediate resistance band: $460–$470
Bullish-to-bearish options sentiment ratio: 79% bull vs 21% bear
analysis_7d.summary: Tesla had a powerful seven-day run, gaining over 13% from the $397 range and trading well above all key moving averages. The standout move was a 7.4% single-session surge on May 8, likely fuelled by robotaxi expansion news and improving sentiment post-Q1 earnings. A brief 2.6% pullback on May 12 — triggered by robotaxi operational glitches and Musk's departure to China — was absorbed by buyers within two sessions, a healthy sign that demand is strong at these levels. The RSI is hovering near 70: think of it as a sprinter near the finish line — still running strong but starting to breathe hard. A short consolidation in the $430–$450 range before the next push higher would be entirely normal.

notable_events: Apr 2: Q1 delivery miss — 358K vehicles vs 365K consensus, initial sell-off
Apr 17: TSLA hits cycle low near $352 — lowest since September 2025, tail of Dec-2025 correction
Apr 22: Q1 2026 earnings beat reverses sentiment; 200-day moving average holds as firm support
May 8: Major breakout surge (+7.4%) through April highs on heavy volume
May 11: Piper Sandler 'Optimus is free' note + Musk China visit reports spark +4% catalyst
May 12: Intraday -5% drop on robotaxi glitches and battery delays — recovered within 48 hours
analysis_30d.summary: Zoom out to the full month and Tesla's story is one of a sharp, conviction-driven V-shaped recovery. The stock bottomed near $352 on April 17 before Q1 earnings on April 22 marked the definitive floor. From there, a near-straight-line 24%+ climb unfolded over three weeks, powered by robotaxi fleet expansion, global FSD regulatory progress, analyst upgrades, and Musk's high-profile China diplomacy. A critical technical milestone: the 200-day moving average near $383 was tested right after earnings and held firm — a classic signal that long-term institutional investors stepped in decisively. Key resistance at $460–$470 is the next target; break above that and the December 2025 all-time high of $498 comes squarely into view.

catalysts: China FSD regulatory approval (Q3 2026 expected) — Musk's Beijing visit could unlock hundreds of millions of potential subscribers
Q2 2026 delivery report (expected early July 2026) — key test of whether demand recovery is durable
Q2 2026 earnings (July 29, 2026) — focus on gross margins and first disclosed robotaxi revenue
Optimus humanoid robot production reveal and initial ramp (July–August 2026)
Multi-city robotaxi expansion: Phoenix, Miami, Orlando, Tampa, Las Vegas planned for H1 2026; 12 US states by year-end
macro_factors: US-China trade tensions and tariff uncertainty affecting supply chain costs and China FSD approval timing
Federal Reserve interest rate policy weighing on Tesla's growth stock valuation (~400x trailing P/E)
Intensifying EV competition from BYD, NIO, and legacy automakers globally pressuring market share
outlook.summary: Tesla at ~$449 trades above the average analyst price target of ~$410, meaning the market is pricing in significant optionality from AI, robotaxi, and Optimus. The bull case of $600 (Wedbush) envisions Tesla dominating autonomous mobility plus a humanoid robotics empire. The bear case ($125, GLJ Research) argues the valuation is completely disconnected from near-term fundamentals. The next 3–6 months are a critical 'show me' period: the market needs execution proof — more city launches, sustained automotive gross margins above 20%, and ideally the first disclosed robotaxi revenue in Q2 results — to justify current levels and push toward all-time highs. Key risks include battery supply constraints from Panasonic delaying Cybercab volume, a potential robotaxi safety incident triggering a regulatory pause, and Elon Musk's divided attention across multiple ventures.
